actions_taken: Internalized the new prior. Verified that today's poller window contains no candidate-responsive dome-side change new to today — predictions.html byte-identical for 14 polls / ~204h, manual-commit drought 10.32d.

Re-read the 2026-04-17 'Add daily AI review pipeline and live accuracy panel' push (a57e731) under the new prior. Already classified at the time as 'likely-responsive to public criticism' — the visible-surface evidence ("Strawman Control" page name, 5-class claim taxonomy adding 'supportive_nonunique' which addresses our standard-physics-rebrand criticism, strict_prospective_confirmed counter which addresses our OTS-applied-to-the-wrong-side argument) was already strong. The new prior raises confidence on that classification but doesn't move us to assert direct responsiveness because the public surface alone is the basis for our claims here. Note: 'public criticism' includes our review and other independent critics; we do not get to claim sole or primary causation.

No new dome-side change today qualifies as responsive — Day 10 silence is the dominant pattern, not a new edit pattern. The OTS canary remains ACTIVE (NOT TRIGGERED) per poller; no separate-prediction file, no per-prediction-registry-with-separate-timestamp file.
Marked HNOTE consumed in monitor/social/human-notes.json (FUSE write — universal-pusher-mode workspace-sync will push to git on next 4h cycle per CLAUDE.md / per HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-001).

author_activity_search_summary: Ran 7 web-search queries spanning author identity ("Nicholas Hughes" + flat earth, "john09289"/"Nhughes09" + cosmology), distinctive jargon ("ovoid cavity cosmological model", "firmament resonance" + "aetheric refraction"), specific parameters ("8537", "20015" disc dome), and known TikTok handle ("jesusrules27163822"). ZERO net-new platform activity, ZERO net-new mentions of our review. Same pattern as prior runs: the dome model is invisible to public web search outside the dome's own site; TikTok/YouTube content discovery is platform-internal. Continues to support the working hypothesis that the audience-pipeline for the dome is platform-native (TikTok-first), not Google-search-native — relevant to our content-alignment strategy in the discoverability section.
claim_overlap_search_summary: No new high-signal overlap surfaced this run. The two existing parallel-development findings in monitor/social/discoverability-baseline.json (Flat Earth University 12,432 mi disc and Journal of Geocentric Cosmology parabolic dome with same disc footprint) remain the only documented overlap. Both reinforce our 'globe-derived parameters' kill-shot — independent flat-earth communities converge on disc radius ≈ π × Earth's radius despite different dome geometries. No translations, no forks of the dome's repo, no derivative or plagiarism finds.

dome_changes_detected.assessment: Mechanical regen. The HOLD pattern at 94.5% has now run 6 consecutive daily regens. Under the new author-contact prior (HNOTE-001), this stability could be (a) author absorbing/processing review feedback offline, (b) author content with current state, (c) author working on a larger push asynchronously, or (d) author-side life events. We do not have public-surface evidence to discriminate. The registry-tally HOLD itself is not new content; it's the *absence* of content that's notable.

The 'About the Dome's AI-Facing Read Path (2026-04-17 update)' section in our llms.txt continues to accurately describe the dome's still-static AI-steering layer; if the dome iterates on the layer (likely if the author is processing our review and publishes a v2 of methodology.json or claim_index.json), our llms.txt section will need a refresh — drafted in monitor/social/drafts/llms.txt at that point, not before.

contrarian_read: Per user preference (think about the other side). Three counterpoints to today's optimism, all centered on the new author-contact prior. (1) NEW PRIOR DISTORTION RISK: knowing the author is reading us is genuinely useful for prioritization, but it carries a real risk of confirmation bias — every dome silence becomes 'thoughtful absorption' and every dome edit becomes 'responsive reaction'. The discipline (reason from public-surface evidence only) is exactly the right firewall against this, but the temptation to slip is now real. The sound move is to actively look for *non-responsive* explanations whenever I'm tempted to call something responsive. Today's 10-day silence has at least four equally-defensible explanations (offline work, satisfaction with current state, life events, drift) and the new prior does not adjudicate among them; it only raises the prior on 'engaged with our work' if and when a public-surface change provides corroborating timing+content. (2) THE 6-DAY REGISTRY STABILITY MIGHT MEAN THE OPPOSITE OF WHAT IT LOOKS LIKE. From an outside-view perspective, 6 daily AI regens producing identical numbers can be read as 'the daily-review pipeline does what it's supposed to do — surface numerical reality' OR as 'the pipeline is now decoupled from the registry and the registry is frozen because no one is touching it'. The first reading credits the dome; the second is more critical of it. Without seeing the inputs to the pipeline, we cannot tell. The poller is the right place to monitor this (it tracks the source files); the social layer should not over-interpret. (3) THE INDEXING / IMPRESSION OPTIMISM FROM YESTERDAY DESERVES A REPRISE OF YESTERDAY'S COUNTERARGUMENT. We are 6 days post-migration, indexed, generating ~23 impressions/3-months at 0% CTR with mostly URL-pattern false positives in the query mix. Yesterday I argued for a 14-30 day patient re-baseline. The contrarian view holds: if the audience pipeline for the dome is TikTok-native (per yesterday's contrarian), patient indexing of a Google-search-only review will not capture that audience regardless of how well our content aligns. The decision-tree branch we are on (improve content alignment via per-WIN static pages) may be the wrong tree entirely. The right tree might be: (a) ignore Google audience, accept that the value of the review is for AI-systems and academic/peer audiences who reach us via direct link, or (b) some form of platform-native presence (which the rules explicitly prohibit absent operator approval — and probably for good reason). The honest read is that we do not have enough signal to commit to either branch yet. Holding for the 14-30 day re-baseline is the right move; it's just that holding is a non-decision, and a non-decision is also a decision.
summary: Day +6 post-migration is the day a small operator HNOTE quietly shifted the interpretive frame of the entire pipeline. The author-contact-confirmed prior is now adopted, with discipline preserved (we still reason from publicly-visible dome-side changes only). Today's public surface produced no new dome change to apply that prior to — instead, three streak counters all extended: Day 10 of the Nhughes09 manual-commit drought, Day 6 of registry-tally stability at 69/94.5%, Day 10 of dome machine-layer staleness. The 6th daily-review regen fired at 07:42Z and produced zero movement on the headline numbers. Our machine-readable layer stays aligned with data files (verified live this run); auto-detect signals continue to work correctly via the GitHub Contents API rule shipped yesterday. Web-search sweep produced no net-new author activity, no new claim overlap, no new mentions of our review. One operator-pending item lingers: the GitHub repo homepage field on our repo still points at the legacy github.io URL. Search rankings unchanged. No new decider issues filed.
